tldr: Both are mature error monitoring platforms; the move from Sentry to Bugsnag is mostly an SDK swap and a re-creation of projects, alerts, and integrations. Historical event data generally does not transfer.
why_switch: Stability score model fits release-quality workflows
Mature SDKs across web, mobile, and server
Simpler product surface than full-Sentry suite
Different commercial terms or support fit
what_to_export: Project list with platform/runtime per project
Source map and debug symbol upload pipeline
Open issue list with assignees and status
export_method: Sentry exposes a documented REST API plus org/project export options in the dashboard. For inventory, list projects, members, teams, alert rules, and integrations through the API and store the results as JSON. To capture issues, page through the issues endpoint per project and dump fields you care about (title, culprit, last seen, assignee, status). Source maps and debug files live in Sentry's release artifacts; re-upload them at the new vendor rather than trying to migrate them. If you self-host Sentry, you also have direct database access for deeper extraction. Cancel only after you have written confirmations and your replacement is live, since access ends at term close.
import_method: In Bugsnag, create one project per Sentry project, picking the matching notifier (browser, Node, iOS, Android, etc.). Install the corresponding @bugsnag SDK, initialize with the API key, and remove the Sentry init from each app. Wire source maps and symbol uploads into CI per Bugsnag docs. Re-create alert rules, stability targets, and notification channels (Slack, email, PagerDuty) to match the Sentry setup. Add team members and assign roles. Run both SDKs in parallel for one to two release cycles to validate ingestion and grouping before turning Sentry off. Historical issues are not migrated; use exported JSON for reference only.
gotchas: Issue grouping differs; same crash may produce different fingerprints, inflating issue counts after switch
Source map and symbol upload must be re-wired in CI; missing artifacts produce unreadable stack traces
Performance/tracing and session replay features are not 1:1; confirm parity before cutover
Annual commitments on either side may prevent immediate savings; align with renewal
Alert rule semantics differ; rebuild rules and verify with synthetic events
User identification and PII scrubbing rules must be re-implemented per SDK

email.subject: Plan to wind down Sentry over the next two weeks
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving our error monitoring to Bugsnag over the next 14 days and will not be renewing our Sentry plan. We're keeping the account active through the end of the current term so we can finish exporting projects, alert rules, integrations, and recent issue metadata via the API.

We plan to dual-report from both SDKs for the first part of that window, then cut over and disable the Sentry init. Could you confirm there is no auto-renewal and share a contact for any final billing questions? We've appreciated the product and the docs.

Thanks,
Your customer
